questions.skill: Describe the structure of an atom
questions.question: Which statement about the structure of an atom is correct?
options: Electrons are found inside protons in the nucleus.

questions.question: What is background radiation?
options: Radiation produced only in nuclear power stations.

Radiation that can only be detected during a nuclear accident.
The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.correctAnswer: The low level of radiation that is always present in the environment.
questions.explanation: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.
questions.senNote: Background radiation comes from natural and artificial sources around us.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which statement about nuclear fission is correct?
options: A small nucleus joins with another small nucleus at low temperature without releasing energy.
Electrons split out of atoms and cause chemical burning.
A neutral atom turns into a charged atom only because it gains heat.
A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.correctAnswer: A large nucleus splits into smaller nuclei and releases energy.
questions.explanation: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.
questions.senNote: In nuclear fission, a large unstable nucleus splits and releases energy.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: Which statement correctly compares contamination and irradiation?
options: They both mean radioactive particles have entered the body.
Irradiation makes every object radioactive forever.
Contamination and irradiation both stop as soon as a lead sheet is used.
Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
questions.correctAnswer: Contamination means unwanted radioactive material is on or in an object, but irradiation means exposure to radiation only.
questions.explanation: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.
questions.senNote: Irradiation does not make an object radioactive. Contamination involves radioactive substances being present on or in the object.

questions.question: What happens to the resistance of a filament lamp when the current through it increases?
options: Its resistance falls to zero because the lamp glows.
Its resistance stays constant because it is ohmic.
Its resistance changes only if the potential difference is zero.
Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.correctAnswer: Its resistance increases because the filament gets hotter.
questions.explanation: As the filament lamp gets hotter, its resistance increases.

A magnetic field is destroyed when iron is placed near it.
A conductor becomes a permanent magnet whenever a battery is removed.
A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.correctAnswer: A potential difference is produced when a conductor cuts through magnetic field lines.
questions.explanation: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.
questions.senNote: Electromagnetic induction happens when a conductor experiences a changing magnetic field.

questions.skill: Describe how to strengthen an electromagnet
questions.question: How can the strength of an electromagnet be increased?
options: Use fewer turns of wire in the coil.
Remove the iron core from the centre.
Reduce the number of cells to lower the current.
Increase the current in the coil.
questions.correctAnswer: Increase the current in the coil.
questions.explanation: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.
questions.senNote: A larger current produces a stronger magnetic field around the coil.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.question: What causes the motor effect?
options: A stationary conductor produces no magnetic field and so creates gravity.
A magnetic field always stops current flowing in a conductor.
The conductor gains mass when current passes through it.
A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: A current-carrying conductor experiences a force in a magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.
questions.senNote: The magnetic field around the conductor interacts with the external magnetic field and produces a force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why an iron core strengthens a solenoid
questions.question: Why does adding an iron core to a solenoid make the field stronger?
options: The core stops the current from flowing through the coil.
The core removes all magnetic poles from the coil.
The core reduces the number of turns in the solenoid.
The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.correctAnswer: The core becomes magnetised and increases the magnetic field.
questions.explanation: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.
questions.senNote: The iron core becomes magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field.

questions.skill: Explain why satellites stay in orbit
questions.question: Why does a satellite stay in orbit around Earth?
options: There is no gravity in space, so the satellite floats in a straight line.
Air resistance above Earth pushes the satellite around the orbit.
The satellite engine must keep firing all the time to hold the orbit.
Gravity provides the centripetal force needed to keep the satellite moving in a curved path.
questions.correctAnswer: Gravity provides the centripetal force needed to keep the satellite moving in a curved path.
questions.explanation: Gravity constantly pulls the satellite towards Earth, changing its direction and keeping it in orbit.
questions.senNote: Gravity constantly pulls the satellite towards Earth, changing its direction and keeping it in orbit.
